options.delta_label_convention: Delta labels (25DC, 25DP, 10DC, 10DP) refer to the BASE currency, USD. 25DC = 25-delta USD call (JPY put); 25DP = 25-delta USD put (JPY call). Negative 25d RR (25DP > 25DC) reflects persistent JPY-call demand — fear of sharp JPY appreciation as carry trades unwind is the dominant skew driver.
options.liquid_strikes: 10D through 10D; one of the most liquid FX vol markets globally
options.illiquidity_note: Extremely liquid in all tenors; 2Y+ is wide market

notes: USD rates well above JPY rates (BOJ normalising slowly from near-zero)
Negative carry for long USD but historically large — USDJPY is the classic carry trade
Forward points are negative (USD at discount); carry cost reduces short-JPY returns

market_structure.skew_driver: carry unwind risk; BOJ policy normalisation surprise; global risk-off triggers sharp JPY rallies
market_structure.liquidity_profile: second most liquid FX pair; 24h liquidity; extremely tight bid-ask

risk_notes: BOJ policy: rate hike surprises trigger large USDJPY moves; BOJ normalisation is the key structural driver
Carry unwind: USDJPY carry unwind episodes are violent and fast — 5-10% moves in days are historical precedent
MOF intervention: Japanese Ministry of Finance intervenes to cap JPY weakness; creates gap risk on topside
Risk-off correlation: JPY is a safe-haven; USDJPY falls sharply in global risk-off events
Option cut: Tokyo cut (15:00 JST) is standard; large barriers are often concentrated at round levels
Real-money hedging: Japanese institutional investors hold large USD assets and hedge via FX forwards, creating structural USD-selling at month-end
